2603.29861.title: Towards Empowering Consumers through Sentence-level Readability Scoring in German ESG Reports
2603.29861.paper_summary: With the ever-growing urgency of sustainability in the economy and society, and the massive stream of information that comes with it, consumers need reliable access to that information. To address this need, companies began publishing so called Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) reports, both voluntarily and forced by law. To serve the public, these reports must be addressed not only to financial experts but also to non-expert audiences. But are they written clearly enough? In this work, we extend an existing sentence-level dataset of German ESG reports with crowdsourced readability annotations. We find that, in general, native speakers perceive sentences in ESG reports as easy to read, but also that readability is subjective. We apply various readability scoring methods and evaluate them regarding their prediction error and correlation with human rankings. Our analysis shows that, while LLM prompting has potential for distinguishing clear from hard-to-read sentences, a small finetuned transformer predicts human readability with the lowest error. Averaging predictions of multiple models can slightly improve the performance at the cost of slower inference.
2603.29861.paper_summary_zh: 隨著經濟和社會對可持續性的需求日益迫切，以及隨之而來的大量信息流，消費者需要可靠的信息來源。為了滿足這一需求，公司開始發布所謂的環境、社會和治理（ESG）報告，這些報告既是自願發布的，也是法律強制要求的。為了服務公眾，這些報告必須針對不僅限於財務專家的讀者，也要考慮到非專業受眾。但它們的寫作是否足夠清晰呢？在這項工作中，我們擴展了一個現有的德國ESG報告的句子級數據集，並添加了眾包的可讀性註釋。我們發現，總體而言，母語人士認為ESG報告中的句子易於閱讀，但可讀性也是主觀的。我們應用了各種可讀性評分方法，並根據它們的預測誤差和與人類排名的相關性進行評估。我們的分析顯示，雖然LLM提示在區分清晰與難以閱讀的句子方面具有潛力，但經過小幅微調的Transformer以最低的誤差預測人類可讀性。平均多個模型的預測可以稍微提高性能，但會以推理速度變慢為代價。
2603.29861.author: Benjamin Josef Schüßler et.al.
